title: Lead Instructional Designer at NASSCOM Foundation

jsonLd.title: Lead Instructional Designer at NASSCOM Foundation
jsonLd.description:
NASSCOM Foundation invites applications from qualified candidates for the position of Instructional Designer at our Kolkata location. This role offers excellent career advancement prospects.

About NASSCOM Foundation:
NASSCOM Foundation is a leading organization in the On-site sector, committed to excellence, innovation, and employee growth. We offer a dynamic work environment where talent is recognized and rewarded.

Key Responsibilities:
• Create curriculum-aligned learning materials and assessments
• Conduct parent-teacher meetings and update parents on student performance
• Conduct doubt-clearing sessions and remedial classes for weak students
• Participate in teacher training and professional development programs
• Participate in school events, workshops, and training programs
• Develop and deliver engaging lesson plans for students
• Collaborate with faculty on curriculum development initiatives

Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Post Graduate Diploma in relevant field
• 0-1 years of relevant experience
• Proficiency in Zoom/Teams
• Proficiency in MS Office
• Proficiency in LMS Platforms
• Proficiency in Research Skills
• Proficiency in Pedagogy
• Proficiency in Communication Skills
• Proficiency in Student Assessment
• Proficiency in Content Creation

Work Mode: On-site - Work from our office

What We Offer:
• Annual paid leaves and casual leaves
• Employee stock options (ESOPs) for eligible roles
• Transportation and conveyance allowance
• Modern office with recreational facilities
• Comprehensive health insurance for self and family
• Competitive salary with performance-linked incentives
• Salary Range: 2.5 LPA - 6 LPA per annum

Location: Kolkata, West Bengal, India

If you are excited about this opportunity, apply now and take the next step in your career journey with NASSCOM Foundation!

educationRequirements.credentialCategory: Post Graduate Diploma in relevant field
